compiled_state.title: pre-commit gates: worktree merge commit invalidates test/review diff_hash -> 3-4 retry cycles per merge
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
When merging a worktree agent branch into the session branch, the pre-commit test gate and review gate should accept test/review recordings from the pre-merge state, or the merge-to-main workflow should handle re-recording automatically.

## Actual Behavior
Every worktree merge commit is blocked by both gates because the post-merge diff hash differs from the pre-merge hash where tests were recorded and review was performed. The orchestrator must manually: (1) re-run record-test-status.sh for each changed source file, (2) clear review artifacts, (3) write new reviewer-findings.json, (4) record-review.sh with new hash, then retry the commit. This adds 3-4 extra tool call cycles per merge.

## Reproduction
1. Dispatch a sub-agent with isolation: worktree
2. Sub-agent commits to its worktree branch
3. Orchestrator runs record-test-status.sh and record-review.sh (pre-merge)
4. Orchestrator runs git merge <worktree-branch>
5. git commit --no-edit fails with 'diff hash mismatch' on both test gate and review gate

## Impact
Each worktree merge in the b885-e9e8 sprint session required 3-4 retry cycles. With 4 merges (Layers 0-3), this consumed ~15 extra tool calls and significant context.
